evidence: 4–6 accounts form a closed transaction loop with $10K–$50K cycled in under 4 hours
Each hop reduces the amount by ~8%, consistent with layering fees in laundering schemes
All timestamps cluster within a tight 2–4 hour window — a hallmark of automated laundering
recommendations: Freeze all accounts in the ring immediately pending SARs filing
Trace the originating deposit to identify the criminal source
Flag all counterparties for enhanced due diligence and 90-day monitoring

evidence: Source account sent exactly $9,000–$9,800 to 5 separate accounts within 60 minutes — just below the $10,000 CTR reporting threshold
All 5 recipient accounts then forwarded funds to a single collection account within 6 hours
Recipient accounts are newly created (< 7 days old), a strong mule account indicator
recommendations: File a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) with FinCEN for structuring activity
Block the collection (sink) account and seize funds pending investigation
Escalate source account for KYC review — structuring is a federal offense under 31 U.S.C. § 5324

evidence: Central mule account received a $20K–$80K deposit then dispersed funds to 10+ accounts within 30 minutes
All receiving accounts were created within the past 7 days — classic synthetic identity fraud profile
Transaction velocity (10+ outbound in 30 min) far exceeds normal retail banking behavior by 20x
recommendations: Block the central mule and all 10+ downstream accounts immediately
Initiate clawback of funds via ACH reversal within the 5-day window
Report to FDIC and coordinate with originating bank's fraud team for network takedown
